{{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町}} and {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient burial mounds. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several burial mounds, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{commonscat|Umami Kyūryō Park}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/toshi/umamikoen/index.html Official site] * {{Instagram|uma9_chuwapark}} * {{Facebook|yamatohanagoyomi|We love やまと花ごよみ}} *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/145067/2017umami_pamphlet.pdf Park brochure]}}

'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町}} and {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient burial mounds. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several burial mounds, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun

'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町}} and {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{commonscat|Umami Kyūryō Park}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/toshi/umamikoen/index.html Official site] * {{Instagram|uma9_chuwapark}} * {{Facebook|yamatohanagoyomi|We love やまと花ごよみ}} *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/145067/2017umami_pamphlet.pdf Park brochure]}}

'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町}} and {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{commonscat|Umami Kyūryō Park}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/toshi/umamikoen/index.html Official site] * {{Instagram|uma9_chuwapark}} * {{Facebook|yamatohanagoyomi|We love やまと花ごよみ}} *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/145067/2017umami_pamphlet.pdf Park brochure]}}

Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==

=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun

'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町}} and {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun

=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|12=draft}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|12=draft}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|12=draft}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|12=draft}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道|12=draft}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|12=draft}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|12=draft}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External links == {{commonscat|Umami Kyūryō Park}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/toshi/umamikoen/index.html Official site] * {{Instagram|uma9_chuwapark}} * {{Facebook|yamatohanagoyomi|We love やまと花ごよみ}} *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/145067/2017umami_pamphlet.pdf Park brochure]}}

{{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|12=draft}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線|12=draft}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|12=draft}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|12=draft}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|12=draft}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|12=draft}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|12=draft}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|12=draft}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道|12=draft}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|12=draft}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|12=draft}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External links == {{commonscat|Umami Kyūryō Park}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/toshi/umamikoen/index.html Official site] * {{Instagram|uma9_chuwapark}} * {{Facebook|yamatohanagoyomi|We love やまと花ごよみ}} *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/145067/2017umami_pamphlet.pdf Park brochure]}}

revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2024年5月}} {{公園 |名称 = 馬見丘陵公園 |英語名称 = |画像 = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg |画像キャプション = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} |国 = |都市 = [[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]・[[河合町]] | 緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =33 |緯度秒 =39.2 | 経度度 =135 |経度分 =44 |経度秒 =16.7 | map = |分類 = 都市公園 |面積 = 65.3[[ヘクタール|ha]] |前身 = |開園 = 2012年全面開園 |運営者 = 奈良県 |年来園者数 = |現況 = |設備・遊具 = |駐車場 = 822台 |バリアフリー = |設計者 = |告示 = |事務所 = 中和公園事務所 |事務所所在地 = 奈良県北葛城郡河合町佐味田2202 |アクセス = [[#交通アクセス]] |備考 = |公式サイト = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''馬見丘陵公園'''（うまみきゅうりょうこうえん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]から[[河合町]]にかけて広がる[[馬見丘陵]]内にある奈良県営の都市[[公園]]。
== 概要 == 馬見丘陵に存在する[[馬見古墳群]]の保全と活用をするために、奈良県土木部が[[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）に奈良公園に次ぐ県内2番目の広域公園として事業に着手した。計画面積65.3[[ヘクタール|ha]]（[[ため池]]・古墳を除くと56.2ha<ref name=馬見丘陵/>）の内、[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）3月末の時点で、47.1haまで事業が進んでいた。[[2012年]]（平成14年）6月、中央エリア及び南エリアの一部を開園し、全面開園した<ref name=馬見丘陵>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm 馬見丘陵公園とは] - 奈良県</ref>。
園内は、緑道エリア・北エリア・中央エリア・南エリアと大きく4つに分かれており、北エリアと中央エリアは離れているが連絡道があり、行き来する事が出来る。
馬見古墳群内に位置する公園のため、園内に多数の[[古墳]]が存在する。
入園は無料であり、付属の駐車場も無料である。しかし、駐車場は年間を通して8時開場、閉場は通常期18時、6月〜8月は19時、11月〜2月は17時となっている。駐車可能台数は臨時を含めると822台だが、休日は近隣住民やハイキングなどで賑わうため満車となる事もしばしある。
== 公園内施設 == === なごみの道 (緑道エリア) === *緑道北口（入口） :[[近鉄田原本線]][[池部駅]]に隣接する。 *緑道エリア園路 *眺めの丘 *豆山橋 *山坊1号橋 *ヤマモモ広場 *山坊2号橋 *山坊3号橋 *三の谷橋 *大野沢橋 *ムラサキシキブ広場 *花笑み橋 :北エリアへの接続路。
=== 北エリア === [[奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|県道14号線]]がエリア中央を貫く形で走っている。 中央エリアや南エリアと違い大芝生広場や木製遊具、砂場など子ども連れの親子が楽しめるエリアとなっている。 *北口（入口） :北エリアの中央に位置し、北駐車場（240台）、北臨時駐車場（60台）やトイレ、喫煙所などがある。 *池上古墳 *前池 *大型遊具 *徒渉池（としょうち） - 噴水 *砂場 *西口 *大型テント - イベントの時には音楽ステージも開かれる。 *集いの丘（大芝生広場） *馬見花苑 - 四季折々の花々に彩られた5,000㎡の大花壇。 *花見茶屋 *カフェレストラン *彩の広場（ダリア園） - 夏にはヒマワリ、秋にはダリアやコスモスが咲き誇る。
=== 中央エリア === [[画像:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|復元されたナガレ山古墳のくびれ部。前方部から見る。]] 馬見丘陵公園の核となるエリア。 史跡である[[ナガレ山古墳]]や[[乙女山古墳]]とともに、バラ園、菖蒲園、あじさい園や睡蓮池など四季折々の花が楽しめる造りになっている。 また、エリアの中央駐車場内にはバス停があり[[近鉄大阪線]][[五位堂駅]]より路線バスが運行されている（平日約30分間隔、休日日中60分間隔）。 *公園館 :2階建てで、1階部分では公園内の古墳や、自然を映像でわかりやすく紹介している。2階部分は展望台となっている。 :また、隣接して204台駐車可能な中央駐車場が存在する。 :その他にも東駐車場（79台）、中央臨時駐車場（143台）がある。 :開館は9時〜17時までで、休館日は月曜日（祝日の場合は翌日以降の平日） *中央口（入口） *ススキの丘 *花の道 *バラ園 *ハナモモの丘 *下池 *上池 *菖蒲園 *けやきの広場 *古墳の丘 *どんぐり広場 *芝生の丘 *流れのある坂道 *睡蓮の池 *だんだん広場 *あじさい園 *あじさいの小径 *柿の木広場 *水分広場 *結びの広場 :[[皇太子徳仁親王]]<!--「皇太子殿下」と合ったものを修正。変更前の文にどの時代の皇太子だか書かれていませんでしたが、公園のできた時期から徳仁親王の事と推測しました。違ったら修正してください。-->夫妻の成婚を記念して造成。広場からは下池、上池そしてその向こうに奈良盆地が一望できる。 *陽だまり広場 *水鳥と花の広場 *未来の広場 *梅林 *カリヨンの丘 *史跡[[ナガレ山古墳]] *[[乙女山古墳]] *別所下古墳 *倉塚古墳 *一本松古墳 *東口（入口） *中央南口（入口）
=== 南エリア === 公園の最南端に位置するエリア。中央エリアとの間には町道が走っているが、スロープ付きの歩道橋（墳観橋）で接続されており問題なく行き来できる。 その他にも狐塚橋、タダオシ橋の2本で中央エリアと繋がっています。 また、このエリアの南部で[[巣山古墳]]と、西部で[[広陵町立竹取公園]]と隣接している。なお竹取公園との間には町道が走るが、スロープ付きの歩道橋で接続されており行き来が可能（竹取公園側の降り口は階段とエレベーター）。 :南駐車場（44台）と南臨時駐車場（52台）がある。 *春まちの丘 *いにしえの丘 *タダオシ池 *狐塚古墳 *三吉２号墳 *南駐車場口（入口）
== 交通アクセス == ===公共交通=== ; 緑道北口 *近鉄田原本線[[池部駅]]より徒歩2分。 ; 中央口 *近鉄大阪線[[五位堂駅]]より[[奈良交通]]33系統「馬見丘陵公園」行きバス、「馬見丘陵公園」下車、徒歩1分。 *近鉄田原本線[[佐味田川駅]]または池部駅より河合町町内巡回ワゴン・すな丸号 東ルート、「馬見丘陵公園前」下車、徒歩1分。 ; 中央南口 *近鉄大阪線[[大和高田駅]]より奈良交通7･12･13系統「竹取公園東」行きバス、「竹取公園東」下車、徒歩5分。 ; 東口 *近鉄大阪線大和高田駅または田原本線[[箸尾駅]]より広陵町コミュニティバス・広陵元気号 北東部支線、「寺戸南口（馬見丘陵公園前）」下車、徒歩3分。 **便数は少ないが、運賃は奈良交通より安価となっている。
===車=== *[[西名阪自動車道]][[法隆寺インターチェンジ]]より[[奈良県道5号大和高田斑鳩線|県道5号線]]を南へ約4km。
== イベント == * [[全国都市緑化フェア|第27回全国都市緑化ならフェア やまと花ごよみ2010]]が、当公園をメイン会場として[[2010年]]（[[平成]]22年）9月18日〜11月14日に開催された。 * [[2014年]]より「馬見チューリップフェア」が毎春催され、県内外から多数の来場者を魅了している。 <gallery mode=packed heights=150px> 2010 National Urban Greening Fair 2.jpg|全国都市緑化ならフェア Umami Tulip Fair 2016.JPG|馬見チューリップフェア2016 </gallery>
== その他 == 公園内にゴミ箱は無く、必然的にゴミは持ち帰る事となる。また、公園内にはやぶが多く存在し[[ニホンマムシ|マムシ]]に注意の看板が多い事にも注意しなければいけない。
== 周辺情報 == *[[竹取公園]] *広陵町立図書館 *[[讃岐神社]] *河合町総合スポーツ公園 *新木山古墳
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Umami Kyūryō Park}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/toshi/umamikoen/index.html 馬見丘陵公園] - 公式サイト * {{Instagram|uma9_chuwapark}} * {{Facebook|yamatohanagoyomi|We love やまと花ごよみ}} *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/61632/ryokka_fair.pdf 第27回全国都市緑化ならフェア（やまと花ごよみ2010）開催結果の概要]}} * [https://drive.google.com/open?id=1LTPi03dJC0D83EsvO6LV9QXjq3K6_PHa&usp=sharingl 奈良県営馬見丘陵公園マップ] *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/145067/2017umami_pamphlet.pdf 奈良県営馬見丘陵公園パンフレット]}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:うまみきゆうりようこうえん}} [[Category:奈良県の公園]] [[Category:広陵町]] [[Category:河合町]]

{{単一の出典|date=2024年5月}} {{公園 |名称 = 馬見丘陵公園 |英語名称 = |画像 = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg |画像キャプション = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} |国 = |都市 = [[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]・[[河合町]] | 緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =33 |緯度秒 =39.2 | 経度度 =135 |経度分 =44 |経度秒 =16.7 | map = |分類 = 都市公園 |面積 = 65.3[[ヘクタール|ha]] |前身 = |開園 = 2012年全面開園 |運営者 = 奈良県 |年来園者数 = |現況 = |設備・遊具 = |駐車場 = 822台 |バリアフリー = |設計者 = |告示 = |事務所 = 中和公園事務所 |事務所所在地 = 奈良県北葛城郡河合町佐味田2202 |アクセス = [[#交通アクセス]] |備考 = |公式サイト = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''馬見丘陵公園'''（うまみきゅうりょうこうえん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]から[[河合町]]にかけて広がる[[馬見丘陵]]内にある奈良県営の都市[[公園]]。
== 概要 == 馬見丘陵に存在する[[馬見古墳群]]の保全と活用をするために、奈良県土木部が[[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）に奈良公園に次ぐ県内2番目の広域公園として事業に着手した。計画面積65.3[[ヘクタール|ha]]（[[ため池]]・古墳を除くと56.2ha<ref name=馬見丘陵/>）の内、[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）3月末の時点で、47.1haまで事業が進んでいた。[[2012年]]（平成14年）6月、中央エリア及び南エリアの一部を開園し、全面開園した<ref name=馬見丘陵>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm 馬見丘陵公園とは] - 奈良県</ref>。
園内は、緑道エリア・北エリア・中央エリア・南エリアと大きく4つに分かれており、北エリアと中央エリアは離れているが連絡道があり、行き来する事が出来る。
馬見古墳群内に位置する公園のため、園内に多数の[[古墳]]が存在する。
入園は無料であり、付属の駐車場も無料である。しかし、駐車場は年間を通して8時開場、閉場は通常期18時、6月〜8月は19時、11月〜2月は17時となっている。駐車可能台数は臨時を含めると822台だが、休日は近隣住民やハイキングなどで賑わうため満車となる事もしばしある。
== 公園内施設 == === なごみの道 (緑道エリア) === *緑道北口（入口） :[[近鉄田原本線]][[池部駅]]に隣接する。 *緑道エリア園路 *眺めの丘 *豆山橋 *山坊1号橋 *ヤマモモ広場 *山坊2号橋 *山坊3号橋 *三の谷橋 *大野沢橋 *ムラサキシキブ広場 *花笑み橋 :北エリアへの接続路。
=== 北エリア === [[奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|県道14号線]]がエリア中央を貫く形で走っている。 中央エリアや南エリアと違い大芝生広場や木製遊具、砂場など子ども連れの親子が楽しめるエリアとなっている。 *北口（入口） :北エリアの中央に位置し、北駐車場（240台）、北臨時駐車場（60台）やトイレ、喫煙所などがある。 *池上古墳 *前池 *大型遊具 *徒渉池（としょうち） - 噴水 *砂場 *西口 *大型テント - イベントの時には音楽ステージも開かれる。 *集いの丘（大芝生広場） *馬見花苑 - 四季折々の花々に彩られた5,000㎡の大花壇。 *花見茶屋 *カフェレストラン *彩の広場（ダリア園） - 夏にはヒマワリ、秋にはダリアやコスモスが咲き誇る。
=== 中央エリア === [[画像:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|復元されたナガレ山古墳のくびれ部。前方部から見る。]] 馬見丘陵公園の核となるエリア。 史跡である[[ナガレ山古墳]]や[[乙女山古墳]]とともに、バラ園、菖蒲園、あじさい園や睡蓮池など四季折々の花が楽しめる造りになっている。 また、エリアの中央駐車場内にはバス停があり[[近鉄大阪線]][[五位堂駅]]より路線バスが運行されている（平日約30分間隔、休日日中60分間隔）。 *公園館 :2階建てで、1階部分では公園内の古墳や、自然を映像でわかりやすく紹介している。2階部分は展望台となっている。 :また、隣接して204台駐車可能な中央駐車場が存在する。 :その他にも東駐車場（79台）、中央臨時駐車場（143台）がある。 :開館は9時〜17時までで、休館日は月曜日（祝日の場合は翌日以降の平日） *中央口（入口） *ススキの丘 *花の道 *バラ園 *ハナモモの丘 *下池 *上池 *菖蒲園 *けやきの広場 *古墳の丘 *どんぐり広場 *芝生の丘 *流れのある坂道 *睡蓮の池 *だんだん広場 *あじさい園 *あじさいの小径 *柿の木広場 *水分広場 *結びの広場 :[[皇太子徳仁親王]]<!--「皇太子殿下」と合ったものを修正。変更前の文にどの時代の皇太子だか書かれていませんでしたが、公園のできた時期から徳仁親王の事と推測しました。違ったら修正してください。-->夫妻の成婚を記念して造成。広場からは下池、上池そしてその向こうに奈良盆地が一望できる。 *陽だまり広場 *水鳥と花の広場 *未来の広場 *梅林 *カリヨンの丘 *史跡[[ナガレ山古墳]] *[[乙女山古墳]] *別所下古墳 *倉塚古墳 *一本松古墳 *東口（入口） *中央南口（入口）
=== 南エリア === 公園の最南端に位置するエリア。中央エリアとの間には町道が走っているが、スロープ付きの歩道橋（墳観橋）で接続されており問題なく行き来できる。 その他にも狐塚橋、タダオシ橋の2本で中央エリアと繋がっています。 また、このエリアの南部で[[巣山古墳]]と、西部で[[広陵町立竹取公園]]と隣接している。なお竹取公園との間には町道が走るが、スロープ付きの歩道橋で接続されており行き来が可能（竹取公園側の降り口は階段とエレベーター）。 :南駐車場（44台）と南臨時駐車場（52台）がある。 *春まちの丘 *いにしえの丘 *タダオシ池 *狐塚古墳 *三吉２号墳 *南駐車場口（入口）
== 交通アクセス == ===公共交通=== ; 緑道北口 *近鉄田原本線[[池部駅]]より徒歩2分。 ; 中央口 *近鉄大阪線[[五位堂駅]]より[[奈良交通]]33系統「馬見丘陵公園」行きバス、「馬見丘陵公園」下車、徒歩1分。 *近鉄田原本線[[佐味田川駅]]または池部駅より河合町町内巡回ワゴン・すな丸号 東ルート、「馬見丘陵公園前」下車、徒歩1分。 ; 中央南口 *近鉄大阪線[[大和高田駅]]より奈良交通7･12･13系統「竹取公園東」行きバス、「竹取公園東」下車、徒歩5分。 ; 東口 *近鉄大阪線大和高田駅または田原本線[[箸尾駅]]より広陵町コミュニティバス・広陵元気号 北東部支線、「寺戸南口（馬見丘陵公園前）」下車、徒歩3分。 **便数は少ないが、運賃は奈良交通より安価となっている。
===車=== *[[西名阪自動車道]][[法隆寺インターチェンジ]]より[[奈良県道5号大和高田斑鳩線|県道5号線]]を南へ約4km。
== イベント == * [[全国都市緑化フェア|第27回全国都市緑化ならフェア やまと花ごよみ2010]]が、当公園をメイン会場として[[2010年]]（[[平成]]22年）9月18日〜11月14日に開催された。 * [[2014年]]より「馬見チューリップフェア」が毎春催され、県内外から多数の来場者を魅了している。 <gallery mode=packed heights=150px> 2010 National Urban Greening Fair 2.jpg|全国都市緑化ならフェア Umami Tulip Fair 2016.JPG|馬見チューリップフェア2016 </gallery>
== その他 == 公園内にゴミ箱は無く、必然的にゴミは持ち帰る事となる。また、公園内にはやぶが多く存在し[[ニホンマムシ|マムシ]]に注意の看板が多い事にも注意しなければいけない。
== 周辺情報 == *[[竹取公園]] *広陵町立図書館 *[[讃岐神社]] *河合町総合スポーツ公園 *新木山古墳
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Umami Kyūryō Park}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/toshi/umamikoen/index.html 馬見丘陵公園] - 公式サイト * {{Instagram|uma9_chuwapark}} * {{Facebook|yamatohanagoyomi|We love やまと花ごよみ}} *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/61632/ryokka_fair.pdf 第27回全国都市緑化ならフェア（やまと花ごよみ2010）開催結果の概要]}} * [https://drive.google.com/open?id=1LTPi03dJC0D83EsvO6LV9QXjq3K6_PHa&usp=sharingl 奈良県営馬見丘陵公園マップ] *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/145067/2017umami_pamphlet.pdf 奈良県営馬見丘陵公園パンフレット]}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:うまみきゆうりようこうえん}} [[Category:奈良県の公園]] [[Category:広陵町]] [[Category:河合町]]

{{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun

{{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.

Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==

revision.text:
{{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai|qq=}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|qq=}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikebe Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|qq=}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|qq=}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomoyama Kofun|qq=}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goidō Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Osaka Line|qq=}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomoyama Kofun|qq=}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|qq=}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikebe Station|qq=}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goidō Station|qq=}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|qq=}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Meihan Expressway|qq=}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==

'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across {{ill|Kōryō|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawai|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai|qq=}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|qq=}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikebe Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|ja|近鉄田原本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line|qq=}}. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|qq=}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomoyama Kofun|qq=}}, along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goidō Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Osaka Line|qq=}}.
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Otomoyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomoyama Kofun|qq=}}
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|qq=}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikebe Station|qq=}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goidō Station|qq=}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|qq=}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Meihan Expressway|qq=}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External links == {{commonscat|Umami Kyūryō Park}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/toshi/umamikoen/index.html Official site] * {{Instagram|uma9_chuwapark}} * {{Facebook|yamatohanagoyomi|We love やまと花ごよみ}} *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/145067/2017umami_pamphlet.pdf Park brochure]}}

The area borders the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Suyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|qq=}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from {{ill|Ikebe Station|ja|池部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikebe Station|qq=}} on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from {{ill|Goidō Station|ja|五位堂駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goidō Station|qq=}} or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|qq=}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nishi-Meihan Expressway|ja|西名阪自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Meihan Expressway|qq=}}.
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
