revision.text:
{{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] and [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|qq=}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line]]. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|qq=}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|qq=}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|qq=}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External links == {{commonscat|Umami Kyūryō Park}} * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/toshi/umamikoen/index.html Official site] * {{Instagram|uma9_chuwapark}} * {{Facebook|yamatohanagoyomi|We love やまと花ごよみ}} *{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/145067/2017umami_pamphlet.pdf Park brochure]}}

== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|qq=}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun

{{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] and [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|qq=}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line]]. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|qq=}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|qq=}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|qq=}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==

=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun

{{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] and [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|qq=}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line]]. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|qq=}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668061]] Wikidata name: Umami Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] and [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|qq=|1=Umami Hills|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361480}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line]]. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|qq=|1=Nara Prefectural Road Route 14|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445275}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=|1=Horyuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11143751}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=|1=National Urban Greening Fair|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11269290}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=|1=Sanuki-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632552}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun

=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|qq=|1=Nara Prefectural Road Route 14|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445275}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=|1=Horyuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11143751}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=|1=National Urban Greening Fair|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11269290}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=|1=Sanuki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632552}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Umami Kyūryō Park]][[ja:馬見丘陵公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668061]] Wikidata name: Umami Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] and [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|qq=|1=Umami Hills|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361480}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line]]. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|qq=|1=Nara Prefectural Road Route 14|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445275}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|qq=|1=Horyuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11143751}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|qq=|1=National Urban Greening Fair|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11269290}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|qq=|1=Sanuki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632552}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Umami Kyūryō Park]][[ja:馬見丘陵公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668061]] Wikidata name: Umami Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] and [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|1=Umami Hills|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361480}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line]]. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|1=Nara Prefectural Road Route 14|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445275}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|1=Horyuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11143751}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|1=National Urban Greening Fair|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11269290}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|1=Sanuki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632552}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==

== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|1=Sanuki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632552}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Umami Kyūryō Park]][[ja:馬見丘陵公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668061]] Wikidata name: Umami Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] and [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|1=Umami Hills|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361480}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line]]. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|1=Nara Prefectural Road Route 14|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445275}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|1=Horyuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11143751}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|1=National Urban Greening Fair|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11269290}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|1=Sanuki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632552}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==

=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|1=Horyuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11143751}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|1=National Urban Greening Fair|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11269290}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Umami Kyūryō Park]][[ja:馬見丘陵公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668061]] Wikidata name: Umami Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] and [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|1=Umami Hills|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361480}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line]]. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|1=Nara Prefectural Road Route 14|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445275}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|1=Horyuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11143751}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|1=National Urban Greening Fair|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11269290}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|1=Sanuki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632552}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun

The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
=== By car === * Approximately 4 km south from the {{ill|Hōryūji Interchange|ja|法隆寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji Interchange|1=Horyuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11143751}} on the [[:en:Nishi-Meihan Expressway|Nishi-Meihan Expressway]].
== Events == * The {{ill|27th National Urban Greening Fair|ja|全国都市緑化フェア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/27th National Urban Greening Fair|1=National Urban Greening Fair|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11269290}} was held here in 2010. * The annual Umami Tulip Fair, starting in 2014, attracts visitors with its vibrant displays.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Takatori Park|ja|竹取公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōryō Town Library|ja|広陵町立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Library|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sanuki Shrine|ja|讃岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Shrine|1=Sanuki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632552}} * {{ill|Kawai Sports Park|ja|河合町総合スポーツ公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Sports Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Shinmokuyama Kofun
== Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Umami Kyūryō Park]][[ja:馬見丘陵公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668061]] Wikidata name: Umami Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox park | name = Umami Kyūryō Park | english_name = | image = 馬見丘陵公園 公園館 2012.6.07 - panoramio.jpg | image_caption = Umami Kyūryō Park Visitor Center{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=11|frame-width=250}} | country = | city = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 33 | latitude_sec = 39.2 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 16.7 | map = | type = Urban park | area = 65.3 ha | created = Fully opened in 2012 | operator = Nara Prefecture | visitors = | status = | facilities = | parking = 822 spaces | accessible = | designer = | announcement = | office = Chūwa Park Office | office_location = 2202 Samita, Kawai Town, Nara Prefecture | public_transport = [[#Access]] | notes = | website = {{Official|http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm}} }}
'''Umami Kyūryō Park''' (馬見丘陵公園, うまみきゅうりょうこうえん) is a Nara Prefecture-operated urban park located across [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] and [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan, within the {{ill|Umami Kyūryō|ja|馬見丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō|1=Umami Hills|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361480}} area.
== Overview == The park was established by the Nara Prefecture Department of Civil Engineering in 1984 to preserve and utilize the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, becoming the second-largest prefectural park after Nara Park. The park spans a planned area of 65.3 ha (excluding ponds and kofun, 56.2 ha)<ref name=UmamiKyūryō>[http://www.pref.nara.jp/21635.htm About Umami Kyūryō Park] - Nara Prefecture</ref>. By March 2008, 47.1 ha of the park had been developed. In June 2012, the central and parts of the southern areas were opened to the public, completing the park's development<ref name=UmamiKyūryō/>.
The park is divided into four main areas: the Greenway Area, North Area, Central Area, and South Area. Although the North and Central Areas are separated, they are connected by a pathway.
Due to its location within the Umami Kofun Cluster, the park contains numerous ancient Kofuns. Admission is free, and parking is also free. Parking lots open at 8:00 AM year-round, with closing times varying: 6:00 PM during regular seasons, 7:00 PM in summer (June–August), and 5:00 PM in winter (November–February). There are 822 parking spaces available, including temporary lots, though the park is often busy on weekends, leading to full lots.
== Park Facilities == === Nagomi Path (Greenway Area) === * Northern entrance : Adjacent to [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line]]. * Greenway trails * Observation Hill * Mameyama Bridge * Sanbou Bridge 1 * Yamamomo Plaza * Sanbou Bridge 2 * Sanbou Bridge 3 * Sannoya Bridge * Oonozawa Bridge * Murasakishikibu Plaza * Hanaemi Bridge : Connector to the North Area.
=== North Area === The {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 14|ja|奈良県道14号桜井田原本王寺線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 14|1=Nara Prefectural Road Route 14|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445275}} runs through the center of this area. The North Area, unlike the Central and South Areas, features large lawns, wooden playground equipment, and sandpits, making it popular with families. * Northern entrance : Centrally located with the North parking lot (240 spaces), temporary parking lot (60 spaces), toilets, and smoking areas. * Ikegami Kofun * Maeike Pond * Large playground * Toshō Pond - Fountain * Sandpit * Western entrance * Large tent - Used for events, including music stages. * Gathering Hill (Large Lawn Plaza) * Umami Flower Garden - A 5,000 m² flower bed showcasing seasonal flowers. * Hanami Teahouse * Café and restaurant * Aya Plaza (Dahlia Garden) - Features sunflowers in summer and dahlias and cosmos in autumn.
=== Central Area === [[File:nagare_tomb_kubire.JPG|thumb|200px|The restored central constriction of Nagareyama Kofun.]] This area is the core of the park, featuring historical sites such as the {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]], along with rose, iris, hydrangea gardens, and a water lily pond. A bus stop is located in the Central Parking Lot, serviced by buses from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].
* Visitor Center : A two-story building showcasing the park's kofun and nature through exhibits. The second floor serves as an observation deck. Adjacent to the center is a parking lot with 204 spaces, with additional lots offering 79 and 143 spaces, respectively. : Open 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; closed Mondays (or the following weekday if Monday is a holiday). * Central entrance * Fields and gardens (e.g., Rose Garden, Hydrangea Garden) * Several Kofuns, including {{ill|Nagareyama Kofun|ja|ナガレ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagareyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomoyama Kofun]]
=== South Area === The southernmost area connects to the Central Area via bridges, including Funkan Bridge and Kitsunezuka Bridge. The area borders the [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja|広陵町立竹取公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō Town Taketori Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == === Public transport === * **Greenway North Entrance**: 2-minute walk from [[:en:Ikebe Station|Ikebe Station]] on the Kintetsu Tawaramoto Line. * **Central Entrance**: Bus services from [[:en:Goidō Station|Goidō Station]] or community wagons offer access to this area. * **Central South Entrance**: Bus stop near the {{ill|Takatori Park East|ja|竹取公園東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatori Park East|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop, a 5-minute walk away.
